author: nflaig
content: noticed the `builder_exists` contract still has the `EXCESS_INHIBITOR`, is that intended?

author: .fjl
content: we have to make a choice

author: .fjl
content: we were able to remove the inhibitor logic for builder_deposits because we added the per-tx fee

author: .fjl
content: basically the fee can only rise before the fork

author: .fjl
content: if we don't implement per-call fee, then the queue could be spammed with fee 1 before the fork

author: nflaig
content: was there a reason not to add per-x fee for builder exits? I just noticed this while reviewing the EIP

author: nflaig
content: imo the per-tx fee makes sense in both contracts, it would also align them  more and we can remove anything regarding `EXCESS_INHIBITOR` from the EIP

author: .fjl
content: I'll do it in a new PR though

author: .fjl
content: For now I want to wrap up the updates for builder_deposits
